response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of solid software engineering experience but the profile is almost entirely web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with no demonstrated background in embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, or the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). C experience is limited to a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel modification, networking protocols (L2/L3, NETCONF, SNMP), or Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement listed as native-level.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong testing/TDD discipline
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on a developer-tools product
Demonstrated ability to work across stacks and pick up new technologies
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency (hard requirement) — only basic German listed alongside English/Romanian
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or network security technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, kernel modification)
C programming experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2014-2015); core stack is web/TypeScript
